mnemonics: CLOCK TICKS PER SECOND (Clock Rate is measured in Hertz, which is cycles per second)
FASTER CLOCK, FASTER INSTRUCTIONS (A higher clock rate typically allows a CPU to execute instructions more quickly)
PIPELINE DEPTH AFFECTS RATE (The depth of the processor pipeline influences the maximum achievable clock rate)
keyPoints: Clock rate is the frequency at which a CPU's clock generator can produce pulses, measured in Hertz (Hz).
It determines how many cycles a CPU can execute per second, directly impacting instruction throughput.
It is inversely related to the critical path delay; a shorter maximum delay allows for a higher clock rate.
commonMistakes: Assuming a higher clock rate always means a faster computer (ignoring factors like instructions per cycle (IPC) and cache).
Confusing clock rate with performance, as different architectures can do more work per cycle.
